summary.intent: Fix two PR review comments on the moltis telegram crate: consolidate duplicate `parse_chat_target` logic into a shared utility and fix `send_typing` to forward `thread_id` for forum topics.
summary.outcome: Created `crates/telegram/src/topic.rs` with a canonical `parse_chat_target` returning `Result<(ChatId, Option<ThreadId>), ParseIntError>`, removed duplicates from `handlers.rs` and `outbound.rs`, fixed `send_typing` to pass `message_thread_id`, all 700 tests passed, and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The telegram crate uses two different error/result types: `crate::Result` (telegram-local error) and `moltis_channels::Result`; shared utilities must return a primitive error type (e.g. `ParseIntError`) so callers in both contexts can use `?` with their own `From` impls.
Telegram's `SendChatAction` API supports `message_thread_id` for forum topic targeting via `SendChatActionSetters`.
The crate had duplicate `parse_chat_target` implementations in `handlers.rs` and `outbound.rs` with inconsistent error handling (`unwrap_or(0)` vs `?`).
A lossy wrapper `parse_chat_target_lossy` was added in `handlers.rs` for infallible call-sites (keyboard/card helpers) that can't propagate errors.

code.finding: New shared module: `parse_chat_target(to: &str) -> Result<(ChatId, Option<ThreadId>), ParseIntError>` — canonical implementation used by both handlers and outbound.
`send_typing` previously discarded `thread_id`; fixed to call `.message_thread_id(thread_id)` on `SendChatAction` when a thread id is present.
`MessageId` import was orphaned after `parse_chat_target` moved to topic module and needed to be removed; `ThreadId` was still required for the lossy wrapper return type.

workflow: Greptile automated review caught both issues (duplicate logic + missing thread_id) before human review.
Returning a primitive error type from shared utilities (rather than a crate-specific error) is the right pattern when the utility is called from modules with different error envelopes.

friction: First compile attempt failed because `parse_chat_target` initially returned `crate::Result` (telegram error), which is not convertible in `moltis_channels::Result` contexts — required a second pass to change the return type to `Result<_, ParseIntError>`.
Infallible call-sites (keyboard helpers) couldn't use the new `Result`-returning shared function directly, requiring an intermediate `parse_chat_target_lossy` wrapper.
Needed multiple grep/read passes to confirm whether `ThreadId` and `MessageId` were still used after the refactor.
open_items: The `parse_chat_target_lossy` wrapper in `handlers.rs` silently swallows parse errors with a default value — could be revisited to surface errors properly if those handlers are ever made fallible.
Verify that all other Telegram API actions that target forum topics (beyond `send_typing`) correctly forward `thread_id`.
